summary: This brief post links to O'Neil's Bloomberg column criticizing Sheryl Sandberg's leadership at Facebook during a crisis period. The post's title suggests that Sandberg's famous 'Lean In' philosophy of ambitious career advancement may be inappropriate when the company faces serious ethical problems. O'Neil implies that Facebook needs moral leadership rather than corporate ladder-climbing mentality. The timing (June 2020) places this during controversies around Facebook's handling of political content and misinformation.
key_points: Sandberg's 'Lean In' career philosophy may be misguided during Facebook's ethical crises
Sometimes stepping back or leaving is more appropriate than pushing forward
Corporate ambition can conflict with ethical responsibility
money_quotes: Sheryl, honey, if this is you leaning in, please lean out
key_insight: The aggressive career ambition celebrated in 'Lean In' becomes a liability when it keeps leaders clinging to positions at ethically compromised companies rather than taking a moral stand.
